data.name: 7. Observability Pipeline — Dual Logging & Usage Tracking
data.value: 7. Observability Pipeline — Dual Logging & Usage Tracking

data.name: Any OpenAI-compatible or Ollama-native client sends a request.
data.value: Any OpenAI-compatible or Ollama-native client sends a request.

data.name: Bearer token or x-api-key is validated.
data.value: Bearer token or x-api-key is validated.

data.name: Both pipelines are fire-and-forget from the request hot-path. If ClickHouse is down or the buffer is full, requests still succeed — logs are simply dropped (DropWrite). The system never blocks a user request to write a log.
data.value: Both pipelines are fire-and-forget from the request hot-path. If ClickHouse is down or the buffer is full, requests still succeed — logs are simply dropped (DropWrite). The system never blocks a user request to write a log.

data.name: but no virtual model with that name and type 'Chat' exists in the database.
data.value: but no virtual model with that name and type 'Chat' exists in the database.

data.name: create it here → New Virtual Model
data.value: create it here → New Virtual Model

data.name: Each API key has its own rate-limit window. Two modes:
data.value: Each API key has its own rate-limit window. Two modes:

data.name: Every request is logged asynchronously without blocking the hot path:
data.value: Every request is logged asynchronously without blocking the hot path:

data.name: Fast in-memory counters avoid DB writes on every request:
data.value: Fast in-memory counters avoid DB writes on every request:

data.name: GPU processes tokens. Once loaded, subsequent requests skip loading → straight to inference.
data.value: The GPU processes tokens. Once loaded, subsequent requests bypass loading → proceeding directly to inference.

data.name: How a request flows through the Ollama Gateway — from client to backend
data.value: How a request flows through the Ollama Gateway — from client to backend

data.name: How long the background health monitor waits when probing /api/tags and /api/ps
data.value: How long the background health monitor waits when probing /api/tags and /api/ps

data.name: How long the gateway waits for the backend to START responding to a user request
data.value: How long the gateway waits for the backend to START responding to a user request

data.name: How long the gateway waits for the backend to START responding. Does NOT include auth, rate-limit queue, or concurrency-slot waiting. Once streaming begins, no time limit applies.
data.value: The duration the gateway waits for the backend to begin responding. This excludes authentication, rate-limit queue, or concurrency-slot waiting times. Once streaming starts, no time limit applies.

data.name: How long the warmup job waits for each model to load. Per-model configurable.
data.value: How long the warmup job waits for each model to load. Configurable per model.

data.name: HTTP request to backend (DNS, TCP handshake, TLS)
data.value: HTTP request to backend (DNS, TCP handshake, TLS)

data.name: Logs include: user, model, provider, tokens, latency, status code, conversation count
data.value: Logs include: user, model, provider, tokens, latency, status code, conversation count

data.name: Model loading time (cold start on GPU)
data.value: Model loading time (cold start on GPU)

data.name: needs another 30 s to finish → completely fine ✅
data.value: requires a further 30 s to complete → perfectly acceptable ✅

data.name: No backends configured for this virtual model.
data.value: No backends configured for this virtual model.

data.name: No concurrency limit — requests pass through immediately
data.value: No concurrency limit — requests pass through immediately

data.name: not found. Create it to see real configuration here.
data.value: not found. Create it to see the actual configuration here.

data.name: Once the backend sends HTTP headers, the timeout is PASSED. The model can stream tokens for hours — only client disconnection stops it.
data.value: Once the backend sends HTTP headers, the timeout has elapsed. The model can stream tokens for hours — only client disconnection stops it.

data.name: Queue until a slot opens — does NOT consume the virtual model timeout.
data.value: Queue until a slot opens — does NOT consume the virtual model timeout.

data.name: Quick admin dashboard ping. Does NOT write to DB — UI-only.
data.value: Quick admin dashboard ping. Does NOT write to DB — UI-only.

data.name: Rate-limiting happens BEFORE the virtual model timeout starts. Queuing here is free.
data.value: Rate limiting occurs BEFORE the virtual model timeout begins. Queuing here is free.

data.name: Reads model weights from disk into GPU VRAM. 27GB on NVMe ≈ 5–15s; on HDD/NAS ≈ 3–10 min.
data.value: Reads model weights from disk into GPU VRAM. 27GB on NVMe ≈ 5–15s; on HDD/NAS ≈ 3–10 min.

data.name: swaps buffers and bulk-updates DB (ApiKeys.UsageCount, VirtualModels.UsageCount, UnderlyingModelUsages)
data.value: swaps buffers and bulk-updates DB (ApiKeys.UsageCount, VirtualModels.UsageCount, UnderlyingModelUsages)

data.name: The global default chat model is set to
data.value: The global default chat model is set to

data.name: The warmup background job pings configured models every 5 min to prevent unloading.
data.value: The warmup background job pings configured models every 5 min to prevent unloading.

data.name: tracks API key usage, virtual model usage, underlying model usage — feeds the Dashboard charts in real-time
data.value: tracks API key usage, virtual model usage, underlying model usage — feeds the Dashboard charts in real-time

data.name: tracks live in-flight requests — powers the "Live Inference" table and Monitor page
data.value: tracks live in-flight requests — powers the "Live Inference" table and Monitor page

data.name: Tries backends from lowest priority to highest. If backend #1 fails or times out, falls back to #2, then #3.
data.value: Tries backends from lowest priority to highest. If backend #1 fails or times out, falls back to #2, then #3.

data.name: Waiting for backend to send HTTP response headers
data.value: Waiting for the backend to send HTTP response headers

data.name: Waiting in the concurrency queue does NOT consume the virtual model timeout. The timeout clock only starts after the slot is acquired. A request will wait indefinitely for its slot — the only way it stops waiting is if the client (browser / SDK) disconnects. A busy GPU does not cause the virtual model to give up on this backend.
data.value: Waiting in the concurrency queue does NOT consume the virtual model timeout. The timeout clock only starts after the slot is acquired. A request will wait indefinitely for its slot — the only way it stops waiting is if the client (browser / SDK) disconnects. A busy GPU does not cause the virtual model to give up on this backend.

data.name: Without specifying a model, the gateway uses
data.value: Without specifying a model, the gateway uses
